name: DynamoDB

unions.name: TransactWrite
unions.comment:
One action for the list passed to `transactWriteItems`.

`TransactWriteDelete` deletes the attribute with the given `key`.

`TransactWritePut` writes (or overwrites) the given `item`, merging
the `key` name/value pairs into `item`, if `key` is not Nothing.
cases: { tableName : DynamoDB.Types.TableName, key : Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.Types.Key, item : DynamoDB.Types.Item }

aliases.name: Request
aliases.comment:
A request that can be turned into a Task by `DynamoDB.send`.

`a` is the type of the successful `Task` result from `DynamoDB.send`.

aliases.name: ScanValue
aliases.comment:
The return value for `scan` and `scanWithMetadata`

Pass `lastEvaluatedKey` to a subsequent call to scan to get more.

This doesn't include `ScannedCount`, because that's the same as
`Count` unless you do filtering, which isn't yet supported.
aliases.type: { count : Basics.Int, items : List.List DynamoDB.Types.Item, lastEvaluatedKey : Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.Types.Key }

aliases.name: TransactGetItem
aliases.comment:
Specify one item for `transactGetItems`.

All attributes are returned if `returnedAttributeNames` is `Nothing`.
aliases.type: { tableName : DynamoDB.Types.TableName, key : DynamoDB.Types.Key, returnedAttributeNames : Maybe.Maybe (List.List String.String) }

aliases.name: TransactGetItemValue
aliases.comment: One of the return values from `transactGetItems`.
aliases.type: { tableName : DynamoDB.Types.TableName, key : DynamoDB.Types.Key, item : Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.Types.Item }

values.name: accountDecoder
values.comment: A `Decoder` for the `Account` type.

values.name: addKeyFields
values.comment: Add key fields to an `Item`.

values.name: decodeAccounts
values.comment: Decode a JSON string encoding a list of `Account`s

values.name: deleteItem
values.comment:
Delete an item from a table. There is no return value.

See <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_DeleteItem.html>

values.name: deleteItemWithMetadata
values.comment: Delete an item from a table, returning the Http request `Metadata`.

values.name: encodeAccount
values.comment: Encode an account as a JSON value.

values.name: getItem
values.comment:
Get an item from a table.

See <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_GetItem.html>

values.name: getItemWithMetadata
values.comment: Get an item, returning the Http request `Metadata`.
values.type: DynamoDB.Types.TableName -> DynamoDB.Types.Key -> DynamoDB.Request ( Http.Metadata, Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.Types.Item )

values.name: itemFloatValue
values.comment:
Return the value of an item's float attribute.

If the attribute does not exist or doesn't have a float value, return `NaN`.

values.name: itemIntValue
values.comment:
Round an item's float value to an integer.

If the attribute does not exist or doesn't have a float value, returns
`NaN` as an integer, which can only be detected via `isNan (toFloat n)`.

values.name: itemStringValue
values.comment:
Return the value of an item's string attribute.

If the attribute does not exist or doesn't have a string value, return `""`.

values.name: keyNames
values.comment: Return a list of the key names in a `Key`.

values.name: makeFullRequest
values.comment:
Request creator with retained `Metadata`

Same as `makeRequest`, but the decoder takes metadata
values.type: String.String -> Json.Encode.Value -> (Http.Metadata -> Json.Decode.Decoder a) -> DynamoDB.Request a

values.name: makeItem
values.comment: Create an `Item` from a list of (key, value) tuples.

values.name: makeRequest
values.comment:
Low-level request creator.

Similar to`AWS.Http.request`, but assumes the HTTP result is JSON and
uses `AWS.Http.awsAppErrDecoder` as the `ErrorDecoder`.
values.type: String.String -> Json.Encode.Value -> Json.Decode.Decoder a -> DynamoDB.Request a

values.name: putItem
values.comment:
Put an item into a table. There is no return value.

See <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_PutItem.html>

values.name: putItemWithMetadata
values.comment: Put an item into a table, returning the Http request `Metadata`.

values.name: readAccounts
values.comment:
Read JSON from a URL and turn it into a list of `Account`s.

If `Nothing` is passed for the first arg (the URL), will use the default of `"accounts.json"`.

You're not going to want to store the secret keys in this JSON in plain text anywhere but your development machine. In applications, they will ususally be stored in `LocalStorage`.

Example JSON:

    [{"name": "Dynamo DB",
      "region": "us-east-1",
      "access-key": "<20-character access key>",
      "secret-key": "<40-character secret key>",
      "tableName": "<your table name>"
     }
    ]

values.name: removeKeyFields
values.comment:
Remove the key fields from an `Item`.

The `Item` that comes back from `getItem` or `getItemWithMetadata` contains the key fields. Sometimes you'd rather not see them there.

values.name: scan
values.comment:
Scan a table for items.

If `Maybe Key` isn't `Nothing`, it's the `ExclusiveStartKey`.

If `Maybe Int` isn't `Nothing`, it's the `Limit`.

See <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_Scan.html>
values.type: DynamoDB.Types.TableName -> Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.Types.Key -> Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int -> DynamoDB.Request (Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.ScanValue)

values.name: scanWithMetadata
values.comment:
Scan a table for items, returning the Http request `Metadata`.

If `Maybe Key` isn't `Nothing`, it's the `ExclusiveStartKey`.

If `Maybe Int` isn't `Nothing`, it's the `Limit`.
values.type: DynamoDB.Types.TableName -> Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.Types.Key -> Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int -> DynamoDB.Request ( Http.Metadata, Maybe.Maybe DynamoDB.ScanValue )

values.name: send
values.comment: Create a `Task` to send a signed request over the wire.
values.type: DynamoDB.Types.Account -> DynamoDB.Request a -> Task.Task DynamoDB.Types.Error a

values.name: transactGetItems
values.comment:
Do a bunch of `TransactGetItem` requests inside a transaction.

Will error if the list has more than 100 elements.

See <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_TransactGetItems.html>

values.name: transactGetItemsWithMetadata
values.comment:
Do a bunch of `TransactGetItem` requests inside a transaction, returning the Http request `Metadata`.

Will error if the list has more than 100 elements.
values.type: List.List DynamoDB.TransactGetItem -> DynamoDB.Request ( Http.Metadata, List.List DynamoDB.TransactGetItemValue )

values.name: transactWriteItems
values.comment:
Do a bunch of `TransactWrite` requests inside a transaction. There is no return value.

Will error if the list has more than 100 elements.

See <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_TransactWriteItems.html>

values.name: transactWriteItemsWithMetadata
values.comment:
Do a bunch of `TransactWrite` requests inside a transaction, returning the Http request `Metdata`.

Will error if the list has more than 100 elements.

name: DynamoDB.EncodeDecode

values.name: attributeValueDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for an `AttributeValue`

values.name: encodeAttributeValue
values.comment: Encode an `AttributeValue`

values.name: encodeItem
values.comment: Encode an `Item`.

values.name: encodeKey
values.comment: Encode a `Key`

values.name: itemDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for an `Item`

values.name: keyDecoder
values.comment: Decoder for a `Key`

name: DynamoDB.Html
comment:
Simple rendering for a list of `DynamoDB.Types.Item`.

@docs renderItemTable

values.name: renderItemTable
values.comment:
Render a table for a list of `Item`s.

The list of strings is the key names which should appear first in the
displayed table.

The wrapper, `(Item -> msg)`, is called when the user clicks on a row.
values.type: (DynamoDB.Types.Item -> msg) -> List.List String.String -> List.List DynamoDB.Types.Item -> Html.Html msg

name: DynamoDB.Types

unions.name: AttributeValue
unions.comment:
A value in a database key or item.

See: <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/APIReference/API_AttributeValue.html>

unions.name: Error
unions.comment:
Errors returned from S3 operations

`HttpError` is from the standard Elm `Http` module.

`AWSError` is from the AWS.Http module.

`DecodeError` denotes a Decoder error in parsing DynamoDB account info.

unions.name: Key
unions.comment: A key for a database item.

aliases.name: Account
aliases.type: { name : String.String, region : Maybe.Maybe String.String, accessKey : String.String, secretKey : String.String, tableName : DynamoDB.Types.TableName }

aliases.name: TableName
aliases.comment: The name of a DynamoDB table.
